key_insight: Raising the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and more wear-resistant, even though friction increased.
engineering_relevance: This suggests spray-parameter tuning can meaningfully improve carbide-based protective coatings for high-wear and high-temperature components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical conditions.
Characterized microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and XRD.
Measured dry sliding tribology in ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating achieved the minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N) and showed reduced porosity versus the 43% coating.
watch_item: The study indicates a tradeoff: better wear resistance at 53% came with a higher coefficient of friction, so application-specific optimization is still needed.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
